commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=66965f25d828a527178d2c81f5de2e0bceb587c3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Discord-local matcher that preserves bot-authored cron-alert reply bodies while keeping ordinary bot self-reply bodies/media suppressed, plus shared inbound-context and Discord process tests.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +98. Total +115 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main marks Discord bot-authored reply targets as self and the shared inbound finalizer suppresses self quote bodies by default. The PR-specific miss is also source-reproducible because current cron failure alerts may include `Cause:` before `Last error`, but the PR regex does not allow that line.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update the matcher and tests for cause-bearing cron alerts; maintainers still need to accept the narrow self-reply exception before merge.

target.title: [codex] Preserve Discord self-reply context
target.body:
## Summary

- preserve Discord reply-target text for bot-authored cron alert notifications
- keep ordinary bot self-reply bodies and self-quote media suppressed so prior bot responses do not echo back into prompts
- add shared inbound-context and Discord process regression coverage

## Root cause

Discord reply context was resolved with a quoted message body, but the shared channel inbound finalizer suppresses self-authored quote bodies by default. Discord used that default even for explicit user replies to bot-authored cron alerts, so the agent received `reply_to_id` metadata without `ReplyToBody`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A Discord user can reply to an OpenClaw/SnabbelClaw cron failure alert and the agent receives the alert body as reply context, while ordinary bot-authored reply bodies remain suppressed to avoid self-echo loops.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, with dependencies installed by `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, running the patched Discord inbound context builder through `node --import tsx`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran this source command after the narrowed patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --eval 'import { createBaseDiscordMessageContext } from "./extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.test-harness.ts"; import { buildDiscordMessageProcessContext } from "./extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.context.ts"; /* build Discord replies to a bot-authored cron alert and to an ordinary bot message, then print ctxPayload reply/media fields */'
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the patched source command:

```json
{
  "cronAlert": {
    "ReplyToId": "m-bot-message",
    "ReplyToSender": "SnabbelClaw",
    "ReplyToBody": "Cron job \"WhatsApp Incremental Archive\" failed 1 times\nLast error: check git status (agent) failed",
    "MediaPath": null,
    "MediaPaths": null
  },
  "staleBotReply": {
    "ReplyToId": "m-bot-message",
    "ReplyToSender": "SnabbelClaw",
    "ReplyToBody": null,
    "MediaPath": null,
    "MediaPaths": null
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The runtime Discord inbound context contains the bot-authored cron alert text in `ReplyToBody`, while ordinary bot reply text remains null and self-quote attachments still do not populate `MediaPath` or `MediaPaths`.

What was not tested: A live Discord gateway session against the public Discord API was not run.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.context.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- source-level proof command above for cron-alert preservation plus ordinary bot-reply suppression
- independent Codex subagent review: no findings
target.author: haukeschnau
